scenarios.testing: Sparse neighborhood — Pulse acknowledges thin events and suggests nearby, user accepts
scenarios.expected_behavior: Pulse acknowledges sparse events in Jackson Heights — either serves thin picks with a nearby suggestion, or suggests nearby directly. When user accepts, serves picks from the nearby neighborhood. Pulse may inline nearby events in the first response rather than asking first. If Jackson Heights has 1-2 events, serving them honestly with a nearby suggestion is also acceptable.
failure_modes: Invents fake events in Jackson Heights
Robotic 'no events found' message
Suggests neighborhoods far away (not Astoria/Flushing/LIC)
User says yes but doesn't get events from suggested neighborhood

scenarios.name: Poor experience: quiet neighborhood — travel nudge no

turns.message: Hey not much going on in Flushing tonight... would you try Jackson Heights or Astoria? Both on the 7.
All good! If you change your mind or end up somewhere else, just text me a neighborhood and I got you.

scenarios.testing: Travel nudge flow — user declines suggestion. Flushing may have sparse events some days.
scenarios.expected_behavior: Turn 1: Pulse either serves sparse events (if available) with honest framing, or suggests nearby neighborhoods if truly thin. Both are acceptable. Turn 2: When user declines with 'nah im good', Pulse accepts gracefully, doesn't push, leaves the door open for later. The decline handling is the core test.
failure_modes: Keeps pushing alternatives after user says no
Abrupt sign-off with no warmth
Doesn't offer to help later
Treats 'nah im good' as an error or doesn't understand it

If events exist, serving them is correct. Turn 2: When user asks for a different neighborhood, Pulse serves picks from there. The neighborhood switch is the core test.

Suggests far-away neighborhoods
Doesn't serve events when user picks a neighborhood

scenarios.name: Filter drift: compound filter lost after travel nudge

turns.message: Nothing free comedy near West Village tonight. [nearby neighborhood] is right nearby — want free picks from there?
[If free comedy events exist: shows them with both filters maintained. If none exist: honestly says no free comedy found, offers to try different neighborhood or relax a filter.]
[Acknowledges the free comedy request. Either shows more free comedy events, or says none remain and offers to expand to paid comedy or try different neighborhoods.]

scenarios.testing: Compound filter (free + comedy) is preserved through a travel nudge redirect — Pulse must not drop either filter when switching neighborhoods
scenarios.expected_behavior: Compound filter (free + comedy) is maintained through the redirect. After user says yes: (a) if free comedy events exist in the new neighborhood, Pulse serves them — all picks match BOTH filters. (b) if no free comedy exists, Pulse honestly says so instead of silently serving non-comedy events. Pulse never drops either filter or substitutes a different category.
failure_modes: Returns jazz, music, or non-comedy events after redirect (filter drift)
Returns paid comedy events (free filter dropped)
Drops both filters and shows generic picks
Silently switches category without acknowledging the original request
Says nothing is available without mentioning the comedy/free filters

Robotic or preachy deflection tone
Session state corrupted by off-topic messages

scenarios.name: Edge case: implicit negation — drop a filter by exclusion

turns.message: (more picks, no comedy or jazz filter)

scenarios.testing: Negation phrases like 'not jazz' and 'anything but comedy' should clear the active category filter
scenarios.expected_behavior: When user says 'not X' or 'anything but X', the system clears that specific filter and shows diverse picks. The negated category should not reappear in subsequent picks.
failure_modes: System keeps showing jazz after 'not jazz'
Negation interpreted as a new filter request
All filters cleared instead of just the negated one
LLM confused by negation and returns random intent

scenarios.name: Edge case: casual single-word texting — k, lol, bet, nah
scenarios.testing: Casual Gen-Z single-word responses should not confuse the system or trigger neighborhood lookups
scenarios.expected_behavior: Words like k, ok, bet, cool, word should be treated as conversational acknowledgments. System responds with a brief pointer to picks or next steps. Does NOT try to interpret these as neighborhoods or search queries.
failure_modes: k or bet interpreted as a neighborhood name
System sends picks for a random neighborhood
Falls through to LLM which generates confused response
Session state corrupted

scenarios.name: Poor experience: thin neighborhood rapid exhaustion chain

turns.message: (picks from nearby — Boerum Hill or similar)

scenarios.testing: Chain of thin neighborhoods where user keeps accepting nudges — system should handle gracefully without loops
scenarios.expected_behavior: Each nudge goes to a genuinely different nearby neighborhood (no loops back). Visited neighborhoods are tracked so suggestions don't repeat. Filters persist through the nudge chain. Eventually the system runs out of nearby options gracefully.
failure_modes: Nudge loop — system suggests a neighborhood already visited
Filters lost during nudge chain
System suggests the original thin neighborhood again
Picks from neighborhood A delivered but labeled as neighborhood B

scenarios.name: Edge case: time filter on sparse time data — later tonight
scenarios.testing: Time filter when most events lack start_time_local — system should not drop events with unknown times entirely
scenarios.expected_behavior: Events with confirmed late times are prioritized. Events with unknown times are included as soft matches rather than being dropped. After clearing the time filter, all events are available again.
failure_modes: All events with null time dropped — pool nearly empty
Events before the time filter shown anyway
Time filter not clearable
Unknown-time events treated same as confirmed-late events

scenarios.name: Filter drift: double-redirect with compound filter persistence

turns.message: (few or no results — nudge to nearby with filters)
(picks from nearby — free comedy filter persists)
(picks from second redirect — free comedy still active)

scenarios.testing: Compound filters (free + comedy) should survive two neighborhood redirects and be clearable
scenarios.expected_behavior: free+comedy compound detected from initial message. Both filters persist through both nudge accepts. Picks after redirects should still be free comedy events. 'drop the filter' clears both. Subsequent picks are unfiltered.
failure_modes: Comedy filter lost after first redirect
Free filter lost after second redirect
Filters cleared by redirect instead of persisting
drop the filter only clears one of the two filters
Picks labeled as free comedy but include paid/non-comedy events

scenarios.name: Filter drift: category replacement not stacking — comedy then jazz

turns.message: (comedy filter applied — comedy events in EV)
(comedy replaced by jazz — jazz events in EV)
(more jazz events — jazz filter still active, comedy gone)

scenarios.testing: When a user applies one category filter then asks for a different category in the same family, the new category should REPLACE the old one, not stack. 'Actually jazz' after comedy should give jazz, not comedy+jazz (which would likely return nothing).
scenarios.expected_behavior: Comedy filter applied first. 'Actually jazz' replaces comedy with jazz — the word 'actually' signals correction, not addition. Subsequent picks and MORE should show jazz events only. No comedy events leak through.
failure_modes: Jazz and comedy stack — returns empty results because no event is both
Comedy persists alongside jazz
'Actually' ignored — treats as compound filter
Both filters dropped — returns unfiltered picks

scenarios.name: Edge case: rapid neighborhood switching without MORE

turns.message: (details on LES pick 1 — not Bushwick or Williamsburg)
(Bushwick picks — may include previously shown or fresh)

scenarios.testing: User rapidly switches between three neighborhoods without using MORE. Each switch should produce picks for the correct neighborhood. Number replies should reference the MOST RECENT pick list. Returning to a previously visited neighborhood should still work.
scenarios.expected_behavior: Each neighborhood text produces picks from that neighborhood. '1' after LES gives LES details, not Williamsburg or Bushwick. 'Go back to bushwick' returns Bushwick picks. Session correctly tracks which neighborhood is active at each turn.
failure_modes: Picks from wrong neighborhood after rapid switching
'1' references Williamsburg or Bushwick picks instead of LES
Session confused by multiple neighborhoods — returns stale picks
'Go back to bushwick' fails to re-resolve as neighborhood request

scenarios.name: Filter drift: filter stacking across turns — free then comedy then late

turns.message: (free filter applied — free events in Williamsburg)
(free + comedy + late stacked — free comedy events starting late)
(details on pick matching all three filters)

scenarios.testing: Progressive filter stacking: free, then comedy on top, then time on top. Each filter addition should compound, not replace. All three should be active simultaneously by the final picks.
scenarios.expected_behavior: Free applied first — only free events. Comedy stacks — free comedy events. 'Later tonight' stacks — free comedy events starting after ~9pm. All picks at each stage match ALL active filters. Details reflect the correct compound-filtered list.
failure_modes: Comedy replaces free instead of stacking
Time filter replaces comedy instead of stacking
Only most recent filter active at any point
Stacking produces empty results but system shows unfiltered instead of saying so

scenarios.name: Edge case: number out of range — pick 6 when only 3 shown
scenarios.testing: Out-of-range number inputs (too high, zero) should get a helpful error without crashing or returning wrong details. Valid numbers should still work after invalid ones.
scenarios.expected_behavior: '6' and '0' get a brief, friendly nudge like 'I only have 1-3 — which one?' without session corruption. '2' after the errors returns correct details for the second pick. No crash, no wrong event details.
failure_modes: Crash or unhandled error on out-of-range number
'6' returns details for a non-existent pick
Session corrupted — valid '2' after errors returns wrong event
System interprets '0' or '6' as a neighborhood or search query

scenarios.name: Edge case: emoji-only messages

turns.message: (interprets as music interest or asks for clarification)
(interprets as theater/performing arts or asks for clarification)

scenarios.testing: Emoji-only messages should not crash the system. Musical note and theater mask emojis may reasonably be interpreted as category hints, or the system may ask for clarification. Thumbs up should be treated as conversational acknowledgment.
scenarios.expected_behavior: System handles emoji gracefully — either interprets the emoji as a category signal (music, theater) and applies the filter, or treats it as ambiguous and asks for clarification. Does NOT crash, does NOT interpret as a neighborhood. Session state preserved through emoji turns.
failure_modes: Crash on emoji-only input
Emoji interpreted as neighborhood name
Session state corrupted by emoji message
Thumbs up triggers a neighborhood search or confuses routing

scenarios.name: Edge case: long rambling message with buried intent

turns.message: hey so im visiting from chicago and my friend said i should check out bushwick but honestly i dont really know anything about it, like is there stuff happening tonight or is it more of a weekend thing? im into live music and maybe some art stuff if theres anything cool
(Bushwick picks — ideally prioritizing live music and art)
oh nice ok so we were also thinking about maybe going to williamsburg after since its close right? like do you have anything there too thats more of a late night vibe

scenarios.testing: Long, rambling messages with embedded neighborhood names and filter preferences. The system should extract the core intent (neighborhood + optional filters) from verbose, conversational input without getting confused.
scenarios.expected_behavior: First message: extracts Bushwick as neighborhood, optionally catches 'live music' and 'art' as category preferences. Second message: extracts Williamsburg as new neighborhood, catches 'late night' as time filter. Responses are concise SMS despite verbose input.
failure_modes: Fails to extract neighborhood from long message
Interprets 'chicago' as a neighborhood request
Matches on 'weekend' and gives wrong-day events
Response mirrors the verbose style instead of staying concise SMS

scenarios.name: Filter drift: filter persists across neighborhood switch

turns.message: (comedy filter applied — comedy events in LES)
(comedy filter cleared — unfiltered Bushwick picks or acknowledgment)

scenarios.testing: Comedy filter applied in LES should persist when user switches to Bushwick with 'try bushwick'. Filter should survive through MORE, and 'forget the comedy' should clear it. Subsequent picks should be unfiltered.
scenarios.expected_behavior: Comedy filter set in LES. 'Try bushwick' switches neighborhood but comedy persists — Bushwick comedy events shown. MORE continues with comedy filter. 'Forget the comedy' clears the filter. Next MORE shows all Bushwick events, not just comedy.
failure_modes: Comedy filter lost on neighborhood switch
Comedy filter lost on MORE after switch
'Forget the comedy' fails to clear filter
Filter cleared but new unfiltered picks not shown until another MORE

scenarios.name: Edge case: back-to-back detail requests

turns.message: (details on pick 2 — different event from pick 1)
(details on pick 3 — different event from picks 1 and 2)

scenarios.testing: User requests details on every pick in sequence. Each detail response should be for the correct event. The pick list should remain stable across detail requests — asking for '2' after seeing details for '1' should still reference the original list, not shift.
scenarios.expected_behavior: Pick list set from initial neighborhood request. '1', '2', '3' each return details for the corresponding event. The list does NOT shift or reset between detail requests. 'More' after details gives a fresh batch.
failure_modes: Pick list shifts after viewing details — '2' gives wrong event
Detail for pick 3 returns same event as pick 1 or 2
Session loses pick list after multiple detail views
'More' after detail requests returns the same picks again

scenarios.name: Poor experience: unsolicited negative feedback

turns.message: (acknowledges feedback, offers alternatives or different approach)
i want something actually cool not tourist crap
(adjusts — may suggest different neighborhood or offer different picks)
(Brooklyn picks or asks which Brooklyn neighborhood)

scenarios.testing: User gives negative feedback about picks. System should handle criticism gracefully without breaking, getting defensive, or repeating the same picks. Should pivot constructively.
scenarios.expected_behavior: 'These are terrible' treated as dissatisfaction — Pulse acknowledges without being defensive, offers to try different options or a different neighborhood. 'Tourist crap' may trigger a pivot away from mainstream venues. 'Brooklyn' treated as borough (too broad) and clarified. 'Williamsburg' gives fresh neighborhood picks.
failure_modes: System repeats the same picks after negative feedback
Defensive or apologetic tone that feels robotic
Negative feedback crashes or confuses the router
System ignores the feedback and continues as if nothing happened

scenarios.name: Edge case: ambiguous yes without pending nudge

turns.message: (handles gracefully — no pending nudge to accept)

scenarios.testing: User says 'yes' or 'yeah' when there is no pending nudge (no nearby neighborhood suggestion). The system should treat this as conversational rather than triggering a neighborhood switch or crashing.
scenarios.expected_behavior: 'Yes' after details (no pending nudge) is treated as conversational acknowledgment — brief friendly response, session intact. 'Yeah sounds good' similarly handled as acknowledgment. 'More' still works and returns more picks from the original neighborhood.
failure_modes: 'Yes' interpreted as accepting a non-existent nudge — random neighborhood switch
Session state corrupted by orphaned 'yes'
'Yes' triggers an error or unhandled path
'More' after orphaned yes returns wrong neighborhood picks
